notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for italy. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

documents.title: Italy Entry Visa and Uniform Schengen Visa

chunks.title: Italy entry visa scope
chunks.content: The Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs describes an entry visa as an authorization placed in the applicant's passport or travel document for entry into the Schengen Area and the Italian Republic. A visa issued by Italian representations abroad allows transit or a short stay up to 90 days in Italy and other Schengen Convention countries as a Uniform Schengen Visa. A long stay over 90 days is a National Visa, not the short-stay tourism route.

chunks.title: Visa for Italy portal
chunks.content: The Italian Ministry points applicants to the official Visa for Italy portal for further entry visa information. For VIZA AI: if Italy is the sole or main destination for a short tourism trip, use Italy/Schengen Type C short-stay guidance and direct users to the official Italian visa portal or the competent Italian representation/VAC for residence-specific instructions.

chunks.title: 中国护照意大利短期旅游签证建议
chunks.content: 中国护照持有人如果去意大利短期旅游，且意大利是唯一目的地或主要停留国家，通常应准备 Italy/Schengen Type C 短期签证材料，而不是美国 B-2、英国 Standard Visitor 或越南电子签。VIZA AI 应先确认国籍、居住地、意大利是否为主目的地、停留天数和是否多国申根行程；常见材料方向包括护照、申根申请表、照片、旅行医疗保险、行程/住宿、资金证明、回程或续程安排、回国约束力和指纹采集。

chunks.title: Italy Type C short-stay duration
chunks.content: Italy's visa type page explains that Uniform Schengen Visa type C is for short stays in the Schengen Area. The maximum duration is 90 days in any 180-day period, calculated from the date of first entry, and a visa may allow one, two, or multiple entries. If the user plans to stay over 90 days in Italy, ask about the purpose and use a long-stay National Visa category instead of Type C tourism.

chunks.title: Where and when to apply for Italy/Schengen
chunks.content: A Schengen visa is for a short temporary visit of up to 90 days in any 180-day period. The EU guidance says to apply at the consulate of the country intended to visit; when visiting multiple Schengen countries, apply to the country where the traveller will spend the longest time, or the first-entry country if stays are equal. Applications should be submitted at least 15 days before travel and no earlier than 6 months before the intended journey.

chunks.title: Italy/Schengen documents, fees, and processing
chunks.content: Common Schengen application documents include a passport valid at least 3 months after planned departure from the Schengen area, a visa application form, ICAO-compliant photo, travel medical insurance covering emergency medical care/hospitalisation/repatriation, purpose-of-stay documents, evidence of financial means and accommodation, evidence of intention to return, and fingerprints. EU guidance lists the adult visa fee as EUR 90 and normal processing as 15 days, extendable up to 45 days if more examination or documents are needed.

documents.title: Italy Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: Italy application channel and form scope
chunks.content: Use the competent Italy consulate or its authorised visa application centre when Italy is the only destination, main destination, longest-stay destination, or first-entry state when stays are equal. Also check the country-specific source: https://www.esteri.it/en/servizi-opportunita/ingressosoggiornoinitalia/visto_ingresso/. Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf ; https://www.esteri.it/en/servizi-opportunita/ingressosoggiornoinitalia/visto_ingresso/ ; https://www.esteri.it/en/servizi-opportunita/ingressosoggiornoinitalia/visto_ingresso/tipologie_visto_durata/ ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen/visa-policy/applying-schengen-visa_en. This RAG chunk is for visitor/tourism-oriented intake and form-filling preparation; route work, study, residence, immigration, journalism or other regulated purposes to a separate country workflow.

chunks.title: Italy fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: The harmonised Schengen form intake should collect surname/current and birth names, first names, date/place/country of birth, current and birth nationality, sex, civil status, parental authority for minors, national ID where applicable, travel document type/number/issue/expiry/issuing authority, home address/email/phone, residence in another country if any, occupation, employer or school, travel purpose, destination Member State(s), first-entry Member State, number of entries requested, intended arrival/departure dates and duration, prior Schengen fingerprints, entry permit for final destination if any, inviting person/company or hotel/accommodation, who pays travel/living costs, EU/EEA/Swiss family member data if applicable, and applicant declaration/signature.

chunks.title: Italy supporting documents and review checklist
chunks.content: Core Schengen evidence includes signed visa application form, passport/travel document valid beyond the planned Schengen exit, photo, travel medical insurance, proof of purpose, itinerary/transport, accommodation or host invitation, evidence of financial means, evidence of intention to return, residence/jurisdiction proof, fee, and biometrics where required. Workflow guardrails: VIZA should determine the competent Schengen state before filling forms. For multi-country trips, compute longest stay or first entry if equal; collect day split and accommodation per country. Do not submit or sign declarations without explicit user confirmation.
